title: senecio yalae
intro: Senecio yalae, also known as 'Cabbage Tree Bush' or 'Pakau', is a plant native to New Zealand, belonging to the daisy family. Its leaves and roots are used in traditional medicine, rich in sesquiterpene lactones, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is used to treat a range of health issues, including colds, fever, and respiratory problems, as well as to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Senecio yalae can be prepared as a tea or infused oil for medicinal purposes, and in some cases, used in traditional remedies.
